summary: Partially decompiled Peach side-B (`ftPe_SpecialS`) by replacing most of the `ftPe_Init.s` assembly block with a new C implementation and header, while leaving several hard-to-match routines as guarded inline assembly under `MUST_MATCH && !WIP`. The PR also performed a large fighter-field naming cleanup across many fighter files, especially replacing raw/old names such as `x2D4_specialAttributes`, `x80_self_vel`, and `x2200_ftcmd_var*` with clearer `dat_attrs`, `self_vel`, and `cmd_vars[]`. Peach dat attributes and motion vars were extended with side-B-specific fields. There was no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: Deleted roughly 865 lines of Peach side-B assembly from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s` and added a new 790-line `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialS.c` implementation.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialS.h` with prototypes for Peach side-B enter, anim, IASA, phys, and collision callbacks, annotated with original addresses such as `11C34C`, `11C4F0`, and `11CB80`.

Implemented core Peach side-B state flow in C: grounded and aerial enter functions, start animation/physics/collision handlers, aerial jump handlers, end animation/physics/collision handlers, and helper transitions such as `enterAirStart`, `enterStart`, `enterAirJump`, `enterEndSmash`, and `enterAirEndSmash`.

Kept several difficult routines under guarded inline assembly for matching, including collision/transition helpers, using `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` plus C alternatives in the `#else` branch.

Extended `ftPe_DatAttrs` in `ftPeach/types.h` with documented offsets for side-B parameters such as `specials_vel_x`, `specials_smash_vel_x`, `specials_vel_y`, `specials_end_vel_x`, and `specials_end_vel_y`.
Added `ftPe_MotionVars.specials.x0` at `fp+2340`, sharing the existing motion-var union slot used by Peach float attack.

Performed broad field-name cleanup across many fighter files: `fp->x2D4_specialAttributes` became `fp->dat_attrs`, `fp->x80_self_vel` became `fp->self_vel`, and individual ftcmd variables such as `x2200_ftcmd_var0` and `x2204_ftcmd_var1` became indexed `fp->cmd_vars[0]` and `fp->cmd_vars[1]`.
Updated central fighter headers/types to support the new names, including `cmd_vars[4]` at `fp+2200` and renamed callback/velocity/dat attribute accesses used by the Peach implementation.
Touched many other fighter special-move files primarily to adopt the renamed fighter fields, not because those moves were newly decompiled in this PR.
smart_moves: Used a partial-decomp strategy: move matched or understandable logic into C while preserving exact assembly for routines that still needed `MUST_MATCH` support.
Wrapped inline assembly with `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` and provided readable C fallback implementations, making the file useful both for matching builds and future cleanup.
Introduced semantically meaningful shared Fighter field names (`dat_attrs`, `self_vel`, `cmd_vars`) before or alongside the Peach decomp, reducing noise from raw offsets in the new code.
Modeled Peach-specific side-B state using `fp->mv.pe.specials.x0` rather than directly writing the raw `fp+2340` slot everywhere.

Named side-B dat attributes by observed behavior where evidence was strong (`specials_vel_x`, `specials_smash_vel_x`, `specials_vel_y`, `specials_end_vel_x`, `specials_end_vel_y`) while leaving uncertain fields as offset-style names such as `x30`, `x34`, `x68`, and `x6C`.
Factored repeated motion-state transition flags into local constants `start_mf` and `end_mf`, making `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` calls easier to audit.
Annotated functions and prototypes with original addresses, preserving traceability back to the removed assembly.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter move decompilation, first improve common struct field names so the move logic can be expressed in terms of behavior instead of offsets.
When a function cannot yet match cleanly, guarded inline assembly plus a readable C fallback is a practical intermediate state; it documents intent without blocking progress.
Shared ftcmd variables are better represented as `cmd_vars[index]` when their per-move semantics differ, rather than inventing global names for each slot.
Motion-var unions should be extended per character/move when a raw slot such as `fp+2340` has a move-local meaning.
Dat attribute structs can safely mix confirmed semantic names with offset-style unknowns; do not over-name fields whose meaning is not supported by behavior.
Original-address comments in headers and static helper declarations are useful for reviewing a large assembly-to-C migration.
Collision flag logic is often hard to match exactly; preserving the original assembly while writing a C equivalent helps future decompilers compare high-level intent against exact codegen.
naming_conventions: `x2D4_specialAttributes` was renamed to `dat_attrs` for fighter special/dat attributes.
`x80_self_vel` was renamed to `self_vel` for fighter self velocity.
`x2200_ftcmd_var0`, `x2204_ftcmd_var1`, `x2208_ftcmd_var2`, and related raw command variables were consolidated as `cmd_vars[0]`, `cmd_vars[1]`, `cmd_vars[2]`, and `cmd_vars[3]`.
Peach side-B motion vars are placed under `fp->mv.pe.specials`, with field `x0` left generic because the exact semantic name was not established.
Peach side-B dat attributes use the `specials_` prefix for identified side-B fields, e.g. `specials_vel_x`, `specials_smash_vel_x`, `specials_vel_y`, `specials_end_vel_x`, and `specials_end_vel_y`.
Uncertain dat-attribute fields remain offset-style (`x30`, `x34`, `x38`, `x3C`, `x40`, `x50`, `x54`, `x58`, `x5C`, `x68`, `x6C`) rather than speculative names.

Static helper names in `ftPe_SpecialS.c` describe transition intent, such as `reset`, `doAirEnd0`, `enterAirStart`, `enterStart`, `enterAirJump`, `enterAirEnd`, `enterEnd`, `enterEndSmash`, and `enterAirEndSmash`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the original `ftPe_SpecialS` assembly block from `ftPe_Init.s` only after adding C and guarded inline-asm replacements in `ftPe_SpecialS.c`.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` dummy stack arrays such as `u8 _[8]`, `u8 _[16]`, and `u8 _[38] = { 0 }` to reproduce stack layout for matching-sensitive functions.
Used `#pragma push`/`#pragma pop` around MWERKS inline assembly routines.
Used `static double const lbl_804D97F0 = -0.0078125` under `MWERKS_GEKKO` to reproduce a floating constant used by collision comparisons.

Kept exact assembly for several collision and state-transition routines while offering equivalent C versions under `#else`; examples include `ftPe_SpecialSStart_Coll`, `ftPe_SpecialAirSStart_Coll`, `ftPe_SpecialAirSJump_Coll`, `enterAirStart`, `enterStart`, and `enterAirEndSmash`.

Maintained original motion IDs through named Peach motion-state constants where available, such as `ftPe_MS_SpecialSStart`, `ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSStart`, `ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSJump`, `ftPe_MS_SpecialSEnd`, `ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSEnd_0`, and `ftPe_MS_SpecialAirSEnd_1`.

Used callback assignments such as `fp->cb.x21EC_callback = reset`, `fp->cb.x21F8_callback = ftCommon_8007F76C`, and `fp->cb.x21F4_callback = doAirEnd0` to model behavior previously visible only as stores to raw callback offsets.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body excerpt contained only an image link, so rationale beyond the title and diff is not available from human text.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully remove the `MUST_MATCH` inline assembly from `ftPe_SpecialS.c`.
Search for later renames of `ftPe_DatAttrs` fields `x30`, `x34`, `x68`, and `x6C` after Peach side-B behavior was better understood.
Search for later PRs that rename `fp->mv.pe.specials.x0` to a semantic boolean.
Check whether `cmd_vars` and `dat_attrs` naming introduced here became the standard across remaining fighter decompilation work.
Look for later cleanup of `ftPe_SpecialS.h` include guards and placeholder includes.
